message:
test(output/context): matrix test pins unambiguous precedence across quiet/json/robot/no-color

The output-mode detector in `src/output/context.rs` resolves the
user-facing output mode from four independent CLI flags:
`--quiet`, `--json`, `--robot`, and `--no-color`. Historically the
precedence rules were explained only in prose at the top of the
module; every reader had to re-derive which flag wins which
combination from the `match` trees below.

Lock the current contract with a matrix test covering all 2^4 = 16
flag combinations so any future edit that changes precedence fails
loudly with the specific combination that diverged.

`detect_mode_global_flag_matrix_has_unambiguous_precedence`:
- Nested `for` loops over `quiet`, `json`, `robot`, `no_color` ∈
  {false, true} construct the argv permutation per iteration
  (including a fixed `sync --status` subcommand so `Cli::parse_from`
  accepts the invocation).
- Parses into `Cli`, calls
  `OutputContext::detect_mode_with_env(&cli, None)`, and asserts:
    * `json || robot`  → `OutputMode::Json` (structured trumps
      quiet and no-color).
    * `!json && !robot && quiet` → `OutputMode::Quiet` (quiet wins
      over the default human path when not structured).
    * Otherwise → `OutputMode::Human` (plain text; `no-color`
      affects styling but not the mode itself).
  The assertion messages include the full 4-tuple so a failure
  names the exact flag combination.
- No detector logic change; pure specification test. Locks in the
  "structured beats quiet beats human; no-color is orthogonal"
  precedence against future precedence-reordering regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
